plan.title: Plan: Build clean.dev Cockpit MVP for AI hygiene and local fleet visibility
plan.overview:
This plan builds the first private clean.dev Cockpit: a mobile-friendly overview of configured local coding projects, git worktrees/branches, pi fleet plans, agents, and tasks. The selected depth is **Internal Tool**: the initial user is Martin, but the clean.dev backend is internet-reachable and handles private operational metadata, so the plan includes proper authentication, device pairing, access control, basic observability, and operator documentation without over-building for public multi-tenant scale.

The architecture is a monorepo implementation with a standalone local daemon in `apps/cockpit-daemon`, shared runtime-validated event schemas in `packages/cockpit-protocol`, and clean.dev backend/UI changes in `apps/web` plus the existing Drizzle/Postgres package under `packages/pm`. The local daemon observes configured projects, inspects git worktrees/branches, reads pi/fleet state files, stores local daemon state and an outbound event queue in SQLite, and streams semantic event batches to clean.dev over an outbound WebSocket after browser-approved device pairing.

The backend stores raw events in Postgres and projects them, with debouncing, into current cockpit state for a mobile-first dashboard. Projects are defined in the clean.dev UI first and mapped locally by project id; repo telemetry is conservative by default and configurable per project. Server-to-local executable commands, remote agent start/stop, issue integrations, background service installation, and full raw telemetry upload are explicitly out of scope for this MVP.
